steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts two things: historians provide knowledge that is “accurate and available for all necessary purposes, but cold and naked, and wholly destitute of the mimic charm produced by landscape painting.” The core tension is between accuracy and engagement. The passage then notes that these defects could be fixed, but historians are “rightfully interdicted” (prevented) from using those methods because doing so would violate their obligation to “biographical exactness.”—that is, their commitment to literal truth.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The correct answer must capture the trade-off between historians' fidelity to truth and the loss of vividness or charm in their work. D states exactly this: historians' commitment to accurate, truthful writing results in work that is less engaging than it could be.

A mischaracterizes the problem as a skill deficit; the passage doesn't say historians lack storytelling ability, only that they are bound by ethical constraints. B is entirely off-topic. C focuses on practical utility rather than the accuracy-versus-engagement tension.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
